summary: Implemented the M7 client design-token and asset-slot foundation without changing simulation authority, protocol, save data, Electron, dependencies, or final art. The UI package now exposes centralized color, spacing, typography, radius, border, elevation, semantic-state, and map-layer tokens; a neutral placeholder-only asset replacement slot registry covers frames, icons, map fills/strokes, route styles, settlement markers, portraits, alerts, settings/language, and tooltips; Storybook has a dedicated M7 token/slot evidence surface for normal, empty, error, extreme, narrow, English, Simplified Chinese layout stress, enabled/disabled/warning/success/danger, and debug on/off states. The map renderer now consumes centralized map render tokens for map layer fills/strokes, route styles, settlement markers, labels, and canvas fallback rendering while remaining rebuildable from read models.

tests_run.result: Modified/new files are limited to allowed UI, map-renderer, Storybook, tests, and message paths.
Task registry lists M7-DESIGN-TOKENS-ASSET-SLOTS-001 as IN_PROGRESS; downstream UI quality tasks remain DRAFT.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-DESIGN-TOKENS-ASSET-SLOTS-001

tests_run.result: Vitest passed: 1 file, 15 tests. Added coverage for design tokens, semantic states, placeholder-only asset slots, Chinese layout stress, debug on/off, empty/error/extreme evidence states, and no WorldState leakage.
Vitest passed: 1 file, 5 tests. Added coverage that map render plans consume centralized map render tokens for layer states.
Web build passed. Vite reported existing large chunk warnings.
Storybook build passed and emitted M7DesignTokensAssetSlots story assets. Existing plugin timing and large chunk warnings remain.

Playwright passed: 15/15. Added M7 design-token flow coverage for Web shell CSS token availability, map renderer routes/zoom state transitions, no approved-final-art authority marker, and Pixi map ownership.
Format check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed.

decisions.decision: Added @monsoon/ui exports for CLIENT_DESIGN_TOKENS, CLIENT_SEMANTIC_STATES, CLIENT_ASSET_REPLACEMENT_SLOTS, slot query helpers, and DesignTokenAssetSlotsView so downstream M7 UI tasks can consume a single token/slot foundation instead of scattering visual constants.

Every asset slot in this task is marked neutral-placeholder. The registry has a typed approved-final-art variant for future reviewed assets, but no final art asset is added or referenced by components.

Map-renderer visual values were moved into MAP_RENDER_TOKENS and consumed by Pixi and canvas fallback rendering. The renderer still receives read-model snapshots and viewport intent only; tokens do not become simulation, protocol, save, or command state.

Added a dedicated Storybook evidence component instead of rewriting the existing app shell. Downstream app shell, map presentation, inspector, appointment, guidance, and regression tasks retain their productization scope.

risks.summary: The existing app shell still contains many pre-existing literal CSS values. This task establishes tokens and CSS variables plus a token evidence surface, but it does not attempt a full shell restyle.
risks.mitigation: Downstream M7 app shell, map, inspector, appointment, and guidance quality tasks should migrate component styling to the exported token names as they touch each surface.

risks.summary: No culture-specific symbols, portraits, scripts, flags, religious imagery, clothing, architecture, music, or ritual motifs are approved by this task.
risks.mitigation: The slot registry keeps those as neutral placeholders and records art/culture/historical/localization review gates for future replacement.

risks.summary: This task adds focused Playwright coverage for Web shell token availability and map state transitions, plus Storybook evidence for token/slot states. Full screenshot/visual regression across every downstream productized UI surface remains outside this foundation task.
risks.mitigation: M7-UI-REGRESSION-MATRIX-001 and productization tasks should extend token/slot visual checks alongside language switching, no-debug-text assertions, and viewport screenshots.

requested_action: Review the token and asset-slot foundation for architecture compliance: no sim-core/protocol/save/Electron/dependency changes, no final art or culture-specific asset approval, no authoritative state shift into React/Pixi/store, map tokens remain presentation-only and rebuildable from read models, and downstream M7 UI tasks can safely consume the centralized token/slot APIs. After systems review, route to qa_reviewer/test_engineer as required by the M7 review flow.
